title: Ritsukos Tour
wikitext:
[]: Ritsuko's Tour             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Jan 27, 2010 8:25 pm It came up recently in one of the many Ritsuko threads of late, and I thought I'd start yet another one as I feel this topic deserves a full discussion in and of itself. What exactly is Ritsuko up to during her "tour" of Terminal Dogma with Misato and Shinji in #23'. Firstly, to review the events of the tour: First we have Ritsuko calling Shinji at home, telling him his restraints on him have been removed and he can go outside now. [wkimg width=200]23_shinji_ritsuko_phone.jpg[/wkimg] We then cut to Terminal Dogma, where Ritsuko finds her passcode no longer works, and is surprised by Misato's gun at her back. Misato forces Ritsuko to show her the secrets of Terminal Dogma, and Shinji is taken as well at Ritsuko's insistence. [wkimg width=200]23 ritsuko tour elevator.jpg[/wkimg] The first stop on the tour is Rei's old room. Ritsuko discusses Rei, but Misato states she did not come to see the room; and Ritsuko responds that she is aware of that. [wkimg width=200]23 ritsuko tour Rei room.jpg[/wkimg] They next move to the Eva Graveyard. Here Ritsuko reveals that this is the place where Yui disappeared, and that Shinji was there at the time. This takes Shinji aback, and Misato is riled. [wkimg width=200]23 ritsuko tour eva graveyard.jpg[/wkimg] The last stop on the tour is the dummy plug system, where Ritsuko reveals the Rei-clones, and gives her monograph on the history of Project-E; revealing in the process that the Evas are "human". She then destroys the Rei-clones. [wkimg width=400]23 C310 dc comp.jpg[/wkimg] Misato pulls her gun on Ritsuko, who begins to break down, regreting out loud her unwise relationship with Gendou. At the end, she asks Misato to kill her. When Misato refuses, she finally breaks down altogether and collapses sobbing. [wkimg width=200]23 ritsuko tour dummy plant collapse.jpg[/wkimg] There are also several other scenes in #23' itself which are substantially related to the tour, but the above is a more contained description of the event itself. As I mentioned in the Ritsuko and Seele thread, I don't think I've ever heard a convincing explanation for what Ritsuko's motivations are here? Why is she giving this tour? What is its purpose? Is she doing Seele's work? Is this a purely personal action? One thing which I believe is clear is that Ritsuko is giving the tour for Shinji's benefit. She releases his restrictions, insists he accompany Misato and herself, outright tells Misato in Rei's room that the tour is not for her, and reveals the secret of his mother's death to him in the graveyard. Shinji appears to be the intended recipient of all this information. But why is the question. Why would Ritsuko tell Shinji all this; and why now? Something which I'm curious about is whether Ritsuko came down to Terminal Dogma alone with Shinji, or whether Misato was with the two all the time. Has Misato simply tailed them down here? I would suspect this, but I'd like some second opinions. Concerning this, If Misato has indeed tailed the two, the what was Ritsuko's original plan with Shinji alone. Moreover, in light of Misato's interruption(if this is indeed the case), why does Ritsuko continue with the tour, and what can this tell us about her plans? Would her actions have been the same if she was with Shinji alone? What is her end goal? What part does destroying the Rei clones play in it? There are a myriad of other questions here, but I'd like to hear what people's explanations and interpretations of these scenes are? What on earth is going on here? What do you think Ritsuko is up to?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Wed Jan 27, 2010 8:50 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Wed Jan 27, 2010 9:46 pm ObsessiveMathsFreaks wrote: Why is she giving this tour? What is its purpose?

[]: Re: Ritsuko's Tour             Quote Post by J_Faulkner » Wed Jan 27, 2010 9:57 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: But why is the question. Why would Ritsuko tell Shinji all this; and why now?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Jan 27, 2010 10:56 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Something which I'm curious about is whether Ritsuko came down to Terminal Dogma alone with Shinji, or whether Misato was with the two all the time. Has Misato simply tailed them down here? I would suspect this, but I'd like some second opinions.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Wed Jan 27, 2010 10:57 pm J_Faulkner wrote: think she would have shot him - in fact I think this would be an excellent ending - because she wanted to torment her fantasy lover Gendo and was emotionally unstable to boot. Then she'd have committed suicide. I think this would be a superb ending to Rebuild and I sorely wish that I was hired as the script writer.

[]:             Quote Post by J_Faulkner » Wed Jan 27, 2010 11:04 pm ran1 wrote: It is generally held that one of Anno's messages was not to commit suicide, if I'm not mistaken, so, though this certainly would be an... interesting narrative convention, I doubt it would particularly fit well in what appears to be one of the meta-themes of the series/films themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Wed Jan 27, 2010 11:09 pm Asuka's mother committed suicide, so Ritsuko killing herself would be a viable storyline. I'd agree that Shinji committing suicide would be stretching it in terms of the message Anno wanted to get across, but it'd still make sense within the story because Shinji was suicidal near the end.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Jan 27, 2010 11:39 pm ran1 wrote: Perhaps she is also reminded that this realization is also similar to her belief that Gendo was probably using Naoko for his own purposes. She thinks that Gendo is a bastardic, evil man, and begins to feel sympathy for the person who has truly suffered most from his personality, Shinji.

[]: Re: Ritsuko's Tour             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jan 28, 2010 12:08 am Wow, tricky question! Why is she giving this tour? What is its purpose? I think she's just trying to break everything she can on her way down, personally. Revenging herself upon Rei (and by extension Gendo) is probably the primary purpose, but why bring Shinji? As I said before I think she wants to drive Shinji away from Rei, but I don't agree with ran1 that there was any sort of positive intent towards him. I think it much more likely that she wanted to hurt Shinji psychologically, either to make him an unfit pilot (pretty serious next to already useless Asuka, no dummy plugs and Rei suddenly indispensable and still likely unable to use 01) or just to goad him into making good on his threat back in 19. If Misato wasn't there to chaperone she might have even tried to kill Shinji. Is she doing Seele's work? Is this a purely personal action? I don't think Seele intended this by any means. It actually had a very good chance of rendering Nerv unable to stop Kaworu or perhaps making 3I impossible if she tried to set off the self destruct at this point. Has Misato simply tailed them down here? Ritsuko clearly didn't expect her to be there. A more interesting question (which I don't have an answer for) is why she is there in the first place. Did Shinji tell her? Did she follow him? Is it possible she didn't even know Shinji was there (and thus she was here for some other reason) until Ritsuko pointed his presence out? why does Ritsuko continue with the tour, and what can this tell us about her plans? She just doesn't care anymore. This is the hand she's been dealt and she's determined to play it to whatever end. She has no hope of winning, and merely wishes to cause as much damage as possible on her way out.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jan 28, 2010 8:14 am I suspect that the tour is to knock Shinji's mental balance off. She knows how fragile the kid is, and she probably thinks that Gendo intends to use Shinji as part of his grand plan. Shinji is Gendo's real, direct link to Yui, and it's Shinji for whom Yui, as Eva-01, is willing to do anything. If Shinji saw Rei as something inhuman and realized that he was the pilot of his own mother, he would totally flip out. I suspect that Misato being along for the ride was why Ritsuko didn't say "Eva-01 has your mother's soul in it" in one sentence. And if she hadn't flipped out altogether (probably driven even crazier by Misato's mere presence), she might have shown Shinji Lilith on the cross and told Shinji where Rei's soul came from. It would have been enough to completely unhinge Shinji. (I forget which episode she says "If Shinji and Rei were to learn Eva's true secrets, they would never forgive us.") With Shinji confronted with the horrors of his father's work, he'd never cooperate with Gendo. And if Shinji was no longer on his father's side, chances would be that Yui/Eva-01 would not be in a cooperative mood either.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jan 28, 2010 9:54 am UrsusArctos wrote: "If Shinji and Rei were to learn Eva's true secrets, they would never forgive us."

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Thu Jan 28, 2010 10:06 am ^Yeah, that was REALLY early on (Ep. 02 or 03 IIRC); Asuka hadn't even been introduced yet. Added to the weirdness was the fact that--even if Rei didn't know the particulars--at that point in the series, she had no reason to care . Shinji hadn't yet started exerting his influence over her. I think the writers were still treating her as the weird-but-not-so-weird First Child rather than the creepy-clone-girl-housing-mother-soul-of-humanity. OMF wrote: Is she doing Seele's work? Is this a purely personal action?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 28, 2010 10:15 am Merridian wrote: ^Yeah, that was REALLY early on (Ep. 02 or 03 IIRC)

[]: Re: Ritsuko's Tour             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jan 28, 2010 2:13 pm I believe Ritsuko may have been referring to the whole gamut of dark secrets of the Evas and project-E, not simply the knowledge of the souls inside. Exactly what would turn Rei against them, I'm not sure. Perhaps Ritsuko—or even Gendou—is ignorant of how much Rei is aware of? NemZ wrote: Ritsuko clearly didn't expect her to be there. A more interesting question (which I don't have an answer for) is why she is there in the first place. Did Shinji tell her? Did she follow him? Is it possible she didn't even know Shinji was there (and thus she was here for some other reason) until Ritsuko pointed his presence out?

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Thu Jan 28, 2010 2:20 pm I always just assumed that she did this because she knew Gendo needed Shinji and Rei to complete his scenario. So in an attempt to exact revenge upon gendo she revealed the horrifying truth to Shinji and destroyed the Rei clones.

[]: Re: Ritsuko's Tour             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Thu Jan 28, 2010 2:23 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Logistically, I think the most reasonable answer is that Misato was at home at the time of the call, and Shinji informed her of it. That said, there are two problems with this. Firstly, we have the scene with Misato decoding the capsule data and with the insert of her gun occurring before Ritsuko returns; possibley during the day, whereas Shinji is called in the evening. Secondly, and more seriously, Shinji has been avoiding Misato since the incident earlier in the episode. Would he really have informed her now if in the very next episode he no longer even wants to go home?

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Thu Jan 28, 2010 5:13 pm She wanted Shinji to know what evas were all about, so he would be more confidant in his mission and less whine, and not only a paw of his father like her.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jan 28, 2010 5:31 pm I don't have any better answers at the moment, but anouther question comes to mind: How much does Gendo know about this little field trip after it occurs? Does he know that Shinji was there and is now aware of all of this? Does he even know Misato was there? Why in the world aren't both of them brought in for some serious questioning as well after getting so close to the big plan, especially if Misato continues to have enough free reign that she can hack the magi later.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Jan 28, 2010 6:01 pm bladerj wrote: She wanted Shinji to know what evas were all about, so he would be more confidant in his mission and less whine, and not only a paw of his father like her.
